name: British Columbia
description: BC-focused guide tracks for societies, with point-in-time source links for date-sensitive checks.

sources.label: BC Societies Regulation and Model Bylaws

rules.summary: Meetings before Nov 28, 2016 belong to the former Society Act track, not the current Societies Act baseline.
rules.tooltipText: Use the former Society Act point-in-time archive for this date. Current BC Societies Act quorum, notice, electronic meeting, and proxy checks should not be applied automatically.
rules.caveatText: This app flags the historical source track only; it does not yet encode every pre-2016 rule.

rules.summary: BC bylaws are the local rule source for meeting procedures, including any quorum greater than the statutory default.
rules.tooltipText: Use this guide to keep society-specific bylaw rules separate from the statutory floor. A bylaw rule version should cite the filed bylaw or amendment that created it.
rules.caveatText: A society's filed bylaws can be stricter where the Act allows.

rules.summary: Bylaw changes are prospective from the filed/effective date, so old meetings should be checked against the rule in force on that date.
rules.tooltipText: When a bylaw change occurs, create a new rule version with its effective date. Do not overwrite the old version used by earlier meetings.
rules.caveatText: Use the actual filing/amendment source when available.

rules.summary: An AGM is generally held each calendar year, and the annual report is filed after the AGM.
rules.tooltipText: For BC annual workflows, check the AGM date, any registrar extension, and the annual report timing against the Act for the meeting year.
rules.caveatText: Registrar extensions and deemed AGMs can change the ordinary workflow.

rules.summary: Notice timing/content rules changed around electronic meeting amendments before the current 2023 wording.
rules.tooltipText: For meetings before May 4, 2023, use the BC Laws point-in-time source for the exact notice wording in force on that date.
rules.caveatText: The app points to the correct historical source track but does not yet encode every notice-delivery variant.

rules.summary: General meeting notice is generally 14 to 60 days before the meeting; bylaws can set at least 7 days, and notice content must include meeting details and special resolution text.
rules.tooltipText: For current BC meetings, ss.77-78 guide notice timing and content. Electronic meetings also need attendance/participation instructions.
rules.caveatText: Bylaws may set a longer or Act-permitted shorter notice period.

rules.summary: Member proposal thresholds are based on voting members, with bylaws able to lower the percentage where allowed.
rules.tooltipText: Use this as the statutory baseline for AGM member-proposal intake, then apply any lower bylaw threshold stored in the bylaw rule version.
rules.caveatText: The proposal wording and delivery rules should be checked against the point-in-time source.

rules.summary: Voting members can requisition a general meeting using the Act's threshold unless bylaws provide a lower percentage.
rules.tooltipText: This guide track supports member-requisition workflows and should be paired with the bylaw rule version for the meeting date.
rules.caveatText: Use the point-in-time source for older requisition wording.

rules.summary: The statutory quorum baseline is 3 voting members unless bylaws require more; if the society has fewer voting members, all voting members form quorum.
rules.tooltipText: Use the meeting date to pick the society's bylaw quorum version. The Act provides the floor; the filed bylaws can require a higher number or formula.
rules.caveatText: Court orders and adjourned-meeting bylaw provisions can affect a particular meeting.

rules.summary: Before the May 20, 2021 amendments, electronic participation used older communication wording and facilitation obligations differed.
rules.tooltipText: For meetings before May 20, 2021, use the point-in-time s.83 text rather than the current electronic-meeting rule.
rules.caveatText: Bylaws may have restricted or shaped electronic participation.

rules.summary: Unless bylaws say otherwise, eligible participants may attend by telephone or other communications medium when participation requirements are met.
rules.tooltipText: Electronic and hybrid meeting checks should cite the rule in force on the meeting date and any bylaw override.
rules.caveatText: Fully electronic meetings carry separate facilitation and notice-instruction requirements.

rules.summary: Voting rights and one-vote rules start in the Act, with class and good-standing limits controlled by bylaws where allowed.
rules.tooltipText: Use this guide when checking who could vote on resolutions before applying bylaw-specific voting-class rules.
rules.caveatText: Voting by mail, delegate, or electronic means depends on bylaw authority.

rules.summary: Before Oct 28, 2021, the proxy rule used older wording but still depended on bylaw permission.
rules.tooltipText: For meetings before Oct 28, 2021, use the point-in-time s.85 text and the bylaw rule version effective on the meeting date.

rules.summary: Proxy voting is not available unless the society's bylaws permit it.
rules.tooltipText: Store proxy permission in the dated bylaw rule version, then cite this statutory guide for the default BC position.
rules.caveatText: A valid proxy appointment still has to meet Act and bylaw requirements.

rules.summary: Special resolutions generally use a 2/3 voting threshold unless the Act or valid bylaws require more.
rules.tooltipText: Use the meeting date's bylaw rule version for any higher threshold, especially bylaw or constitution amendments.
rules.caveatText: Some actions have special statutory rules or limits on higher thresholds.

rules.summary: Society records, maintenance, and inspection rights are statutory obligations that should stay outside bylaw-only configuration.
rules.tooltipText: Use this track for records and source-document audit checks rather than treating records access as only a society preference.
rules.caveatText: Access restrictions and fees can vary by record type and requester.

rules.summary: The BC Model Bylaws quorum is 3 voting members or 10% of voting members, whichever is greater.
rules.tooltipText: Show this only as a model-bylaw guide unless the society actually adopted the Model Bylaws or matching wording.
rules.caveatText: The society's own filed bylaws control if they differ.

rules.summary: The BC Model Bylaws do not permit proxy voting.
rules.tooltipText: Use this guide only if the society adopted the Model Bylaws or uses equivalent proxy wording.
rules.caveatText: Other BC bylaws may permit proxies because the Act allows bylaw permission.

rules.summary: The BC Model Bylaws directors' meeting quorum is a majority of directors.
rules.tooltipText: Use this guide only if the society adopted the Model Bylaws or matching directors' quorum wording.
rules.caveatText: The society's board-meeting bylaw language controls if different.
